title: WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM - 211info
text: SALUD MEDICAL CENTER WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF WIC is a public health program designed to improve health outcomes and influence lifetime nutrition and health behaviors for women, infants, and children. Nutrition education classes are provided as part of the program; other services include breastfeeding support, monthly benefits for supplemental nutritious foods, and referrals to community resources. 1175 Mt Hood Avenue Woodburn, OR 97071 Eligibility: Applicants must meet four criteria to be eligible for WIC: -Live in Oregon (US citizenship is NOT required to participate in WIC) -Be a pregnant, postpartum, or breastfeeding woman, or an infant or child under age 5. Fathers, foster parents, and other guardians can also apply on behalf of a qualifying child. -Have a household income below 185% of the federal poverty level (FPL). Families receiving OHP, SNAP, or TANF are automatically income-eligible for WIC. -Have a nutritional risk (assessed at time of appointment) Hours: Monday-Thursday 7:30am-7pm, Friday 7:30am-5pm, Saturday 8am-11:40am adn 12:40pm-5pm (503) 982-2000 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 982-2000 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information or appointment FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Proof of ID for parent or guardian and child, proof of address, and proof of income LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, Russian, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Marion and Yamhill Counties Services: WIC Other Services Offered At This Location: HEALTH SCREENING/DIAGNOSTIC SERVICES - SCREENWISE-ENROLLING HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - CHRONIC DISEASE AND DIABETES SELF-MANAGEMENT HUMAN REPRODUCTION - COMMUNITY HEALTH SERVICES OUTPATIENT HEALTH FACILITIES - GENERAL MEDICAL CARE OREGON SPECIALTY MEDICINE - DENTAL CARE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. SALUD MEDICAL CENTER
